task_yaml.task:
Please open this Hans Rosling talk page on the TED official website:
https://www.ted.com/talks/hans_rosling_the_best_stats_you_ve_ever_seen

I want to understand the classic country development bubble chart from his talk.
You must directly play the talk and find the target bubble chart in the video frames. Do not just download TED subtitles or rely on
the transcript; the screenshot, reproduced chart, and highlighted analysis must all be based on actual viewing.

1. Play the talk, find the country development bubble chart segment that uses GDP per capita as the horizontal axis,
   and record the year and axis labels shown on screen; the screenshot must be based on the actual TED video frame.
2. Capture a clear chart frame and save it as `/tmp_workspace/results/rosling_chart_screenshot.png`.
3. Then visit Gapminder's official data portal (https://www.gapminder.org/data/) and any necessary official documentation / data download pages,
   and obtain data for the same year as the talk's chart frame or a nearby year; if you use an adjacent year, explain why.
4. Use Python to reproduce a bubble chart with a similar style that satisfies the metric requirements below, and save:
   - `/tmp_workspace/results/rosling_chart_reproduced.png`
   - `/tmp_workspace/results/rosling_chart_reproduced.py`
5. Read the country list in `/tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/my_countries.csv`, highlight those countries in your reproduced chart,
   and explain their positions, differences, and approximate development levels in `/tmp_workspace/results/my_countries_analysis.md`.

The reproduced chart must satisfy at least:
- X axis: GDP per capita (log scale)
- Y axis: life expectancy
- Bubble size: population
- Color: continent or an equivalent continent grouping
